title: RBI Monetary Policy 2026 Highlights: India still in same sweet ‘goldilocks’ spot, growth looking even better, says Sanjay Malhotra
summary: RBI MPC Meet 2026 Highlights: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Friday announced that it has unanimously voted to keep its key repo rate steady at 5.25%, sticking to its pause on interest rates even as India’s growth outlook improves after a strong policy week. Inflation is not a big worry right now and RBI noted that most everyday prices are stable. However, RBI has raised its inflation targets slightly. The small rise in the RBI’s inflation outlook is mainly because gold and silver have become more expensive, not because daily essentials are shooting up. On growth, the RBI sees India’s economy staying strong, driven largely by domestic demand. Trade deals and government spending are helping, even as global risks continue. The decision comes after the Union Budget boosted government spending and US President Donald Trump announced a trade deal with New Delhi, both of which have lifted expectations for faster economic growth. With fiscal support rising and external trade prospects improving, markets are now focused on how the RBI balances inflation risks with growth momentum.

title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.15% amid accelerating inflation

summary: RBI MPC statement: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate at 5.25% and a neutral stance, prioritizing stability amidst global uncertainties.
